    • Gino W.

      Verified

      Reviewed 2/12/26, United States.

      5/5

      Disclaimer: I'm an iOS man. I have every Apple device possible. lol I purchased the Pixel 8 to use as a testing device for android apps and I really love it. It looks brand new and is exactly what was described. There are no scratches, scrapes or cracks. I recommend getting a case though. You wouldn't want to drop this beauty. I'm extremely satisfied with this purchase and it has changed my views on buying refurbished items. I was a bit skeptical at first but, I'm happy that my doubts were misplaced.

    Everything you ever wanted to know about the Google Pixel 8 128GB - Black - Unlocked

    • Durability

    • Overall performance

    • Camera

    • Screen quality

    • Google Pixel 8Model

    • UnlockedCarrier compatibility

    • Google PixelSerie

    • Physical SIM + eSIMSIM card

    • 50 megapixelsMain camera

    • GE2AEManufacturer Ref.

    • 7 ozWeight

    • 8 GBMemory (GB)

    • USB-CConnector

    The Google Pixel 8 steps up as Google’s latest standard flagship, pushing software smarts and a solid camera in a clean, compact package. Released in October 2023, it’s one generation back from the latest but keeps the perks rolling with Google’s AI tools and thoughtful design upgrades. If you’re riding into 2025 and want a phone that knows what you need (before you do), this pixel might just hit the sweet spot—literally and figuratively.

    Key features:
    • Release Date: October 2023
    • Display: 6.2-inch OLED, FHD+ (2400 x 1080 px) with 120Hz refresh rate
    • Processor: Google Tensor G3 chipset
    • Camera: Dual rear system — 50MP wide, 12MP ultrawide
    • Operating System: Ships with Android 14
    • Wi-Fi: Wi-Fi 7 support
    • Water & Dust Resistance: IP68 rating
    Who is this for?

    The Google Pixel 8 is built for folks who want pure Android, seamless Google integration, and AI-powered photo tools—think students, Google loyalists, or anyone ready to ditch the digital clutter for something straightforward. Compared to the Pixel 7, the Pixel 8 brings a smoother screen and a little extra AI magic, but the real changes are subtle. If you don’t need zoom-crazy camera hardware (hello, Pro models), this is a smart, reliable pre-owned device with a user-friendly vibe.

    Pros & Cons:
    Pros Cons
    Compact form factor comfortable for one-handed use. No telephoto lens for detailed zoom shots.
    Vibrant OLED display with 120Hz refresh rate. Minimal hardware changes over previous generation.
    Pure Android experience with Google’s latest features and long software support. Can feel basic compared to some competing flagships (no periscope lens, no ultra-premium build).

    Why refurbished?

    Going for a refurbished Google Pixel 8 keeps tech in the loop and waste out of landfills—a choice that helps you, your wallet, and the planet. Every pre-owned device from Back Market is inspected and tested to meet rigorous standards, so you get reliable performance without the new-phone smell (or the unnecessary packaging). Learn more about our quality standards.
